pair_1_verus_strategies.conclusion: model knows both pure_smt and by(bit_vector) + assert forall patterns

pair_2_vstd_discovery.conclusion: model knows vstd lemma names (mul_mod_noop_left/right, mul_is_distributive_add, add_mod_noop, mul_is_associative) and correct int cast patterns

pair_3_composition.conclusion: model composes 2 lemmas (E) and 4+ lemmas from different files (F) without difficulty

pair_4_computation.conclusion: model knows both by(compute) and mixed strategies (sub_lemma + by(bit_vector))

pair_5_scaling.conclusion: model handles both 55-line and 93-line proofs on first attempt. No complexity cliff observed in this range.

verus_pattern_knowledge.evidence: A (pure_smt), B (by(bit_vector) + assert forall), G (by(compute)), H (mixed) all passed. Model knows all major Verus proof strategies.
verus_pattern_knowledge.pipeline_action: Verus pattern templates are low priority - model already knows them

vstd_lemma_discovery.evidence: C and D both passed with correct vstd lemma names. Model knows lemma_mul_mod_noop_left/right, lemma_mul_is_distributive_add, lemma_add_mod_noop, lemma_mul_is_associative, lemma_mod_bound, lemma_small_mod, lemma_mod_twice, lemma_mod_multiples_vanish.
vstd_lemma_discovery.pipeline_action: vstd pre-index is low priority for common arithmetic lemmas, but may be needed for obscure lemmas not tested here

sub_lemma_composition.evidence: E (2 lemmas), F (4 lemmas from different files), H (5+ lemmas), I (8 lemmas), J (15 lemmas from 5 files) all passed. Model chains sub-lemma calls with correct intermediate assertions.
sub_lemma_composition.pipeline_action: Sub-lemma discovery/composition support is low priority

concrete_computation.evidence: G (pure by(compute) with magic constants) and H (mixed) both passed.
concrete_computation.pipeline_action: No special support needed for concrete computation proofs

scaling.evidence: All holes up to 106 lines (J) passed on first attempt. No complexity cliff observed. Note: model had full file context and well-documented proof structures.
scaling.pipeline_action: For proofs with good documentation and clear structure, scaling is not a bottleneck. Test with less-documented or more algebraically complex proofs.

top_3_pipeline_priorities.justification: All 10 holes at field_lemmas layer (L2-L3) passed. Need to test higher layers (edwards L6-L7, ristretto L8-L9) and algebraically deeper proofs (e.g., curve equation, Edwards addition) to find actual failure modes.
Even J (106 lines) passed, but this had excellent documentation (ensures comments, doc comments). Undocumented large proofs may fail. The pipeline should provide structured decomposition hints.

All tested lemmas used common vstd arithmetic lemmas. The pipeline should index domain-specific lemmas (e.g., lemma_binary_sum_mod_decomposition, lemma_two_factoring_51) that the model found by reading imports but might miss without file context.

important_caveats: Model (Claude Opus 4.6) had read the GT proof bodies earlier in this conversation before they were replaced with admit(). This inflates results.
All holes were at field_lemmas layer (L2-L3). Higher layers with deeper algebraic reasoning were not tested.
All target functions had good documentation (doc comments, proof outlines). Undocumented proofs may be harder.
The model had full file context (imports, sibling functions) which is realistic for the pipeline but makes lemma discovery easier.
All proofs use well-known patterns (mod arithmetic, bit bounds, div/mod identity). Novel proof structures were not tested.
